attributes.action: Create a free and accessible NHS period tracking app
attributes.additional_details: Many people rely on period tracking apps to manage menstrual health, monitor symptoms and track irregular cycles. However, many apps charge subscriptions for features such as symptom analysis and cycle predictions, or raise concerns around privacy and personal health data. Some apps may also provide unreliable information. A free NHS app could provide reliable information and guidance for conditions such as endometriosis and PCOS, helping people better understand symptoms and next steps.
attributes.background: I would like the UK Government to create a free and accessible NHS period tracking app. Many existing apps require subscriptions for important features, making menstrual health support less accessible for those in poverty. A free NHS app could provide secure cycle tracking and health information.

rejection.details:
Your petition is about something that the Scottish Government and Scottish Parliament is responsible for. That means that the UK Government and Parliament can't look into it. 

The Scottish Parliament has its own Public Petitions Committee. If you'd like to send your petition to the Scottish Parliament, you can find out more here: https://petitions.parliament.scot/

You may also wish to contact your local council and MSP who represent you about this issue. 

You can find the name of your local council and how to contact them here: https://www.mygov.scot/find-your-local-council

You can find the name of your local MSP and how to contact them here: https://www.parliament.scot/msps
